figures.caption: Table 1. Quantitative comparison on Dycheck iPhone [47]. Each cell shows mPSNR / mSSIM / mLPIPS / Stream(MB). Values in red mark the best result and values in green the second-best result for each per-column per-metric entry.

Figure 3. Qualitative comparison on Dycheck iPhone. Four scenes (apple, spin, paper-windmill, wheel; vertical labels) in a 2×2 grid; within each block: GT, MoDec-GS (18.37 MB), Ours (6.88 MB). Red boxes mark the region of interest, identical across columns. At ∼62% smaller bitstream, Ours matches MoDec-GS on static-leaning/high-frequency-edge cases and inherits the same residual motion artefacts on the hardest fast-rotation case (spin).

Figure 2. PD-4DGS pipeline. (a) Attribute R-DO compresses anchor attributes through a learnable mask mi and a Gaussian entropy model, while temporal mask consistency (TMC) stabilises mi over time; the gated anchor set A′ is the input to (b). (b) HDD factorises the rendering network into three additive bitstreams—Static Scaffold (ss0), +Global Deformation (ss1), +Local Refinement (ss2). (c) A capacity-weighted sampler ℓ ∼ π(ρ), driven online by the mask-activation rate ρ, shifts training budget toward deeper layers on motion-dense scenes; the multiplexer routes the chosen bitstream to the rasteriser, producing Îℓ.
